hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 33.4%, Arizona is priced as a top-8 contender, which tracks with their tournament pedigree but ignores a brutal Pac-12 rebuild that's leaving them undershooting their historical ceiling. The real tell is the $1.6M volume and 2.2-point 24h pump, suggesting smart money is buying the brand name ahead of a spring run that could easily derail if their guard play stays inconsistent.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's tournament odds just jumped. Is the market pricing in recruiting momentum or overreacting?
daily_take.deck: Arizona's 2026 NCAA Tournament win probability jumped 2.2 points to 33.4% on $1.6M volume, signaling real money flowing in on the Wildcats. The timing matters: this tracks with late-cycle recruiting noise and the portal getting active. The sharp question is whether 33% reflects genuine title contention or just seasonal optimism. A team at 33% needs to be a legitimate Final Four threat. Arizona's roster trajectory and recruiting class suggest they're closer to a Sweet 16 team. Bet against the enthusiasm here.

sidebar.headline: Rubio's nomination odds climbed 2.4 points in a crowded Republican field. Trump's shadow looms larger than the market's pricing suggests.
sidebar.label: Marco Rubio 2028: 21.1%

sidebar.headline: McIlroy at 6.5% to win the Masters is criminally low for a guy who can still make five more runs at it. Course fit matters, aging doesn't destroy golf swings overnight.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy 2026: 6.5%

sidebar.headline: SpaceX IPO odds just dropped 1.5 points despite Elon's public signals. Thin volume means the market is barely paying attention to this one yet.
sidebar.label: SpaceX IPO Announcement: 22.0%
